name: Michael Wolff
abstract: Michael Blieden Wolff (born July 31, 1952) is an American jazz pianist, composer, and music producer. He became known for serving as the bandleader and musical director of The Arsenio Hall Show (1989–94).

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Nancy Wilson 70th Birthday Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: you did a 1981 trio album called At My Best with Mike Wolff... Michael was with me five or six years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy McCurdy
about_quotes.evidence: and then there was Mike Wolff came on the band... But he was our last piano player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roy McCurdy interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Michael Wolff was not with me then but had been with me for five years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I did a recording with the boys, the little boy – Michael Wolff’s two little boys... We went in the studio, me and Michael and John B. and Roy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
